mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day forward returns across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during late-cycle expansions signal peak output capacity that precedes economic slowdowns and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction in early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase surpassing 0.4 percent triggers immediate inflation fears that compress equity valuations and generate negative returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that boosts corporate revenue expectations and yields positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces a rank IC below 0.01 over 60-day horizons or fails to outperform a baseline model during labor tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent reflects sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than half of observed quarters, or rank IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate hikes during periods of high market volatility trigger liquidity shocks that force rapid deleveraging and produce negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rate increases during high volatility periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal shows no directional bias compared to rate hikes in low volatility regimes.
